title: Map context decides whether a greedy-looking play is actually safe
body: Some plays look greedy only if you ignore the map. Staying on a wave at low HP, pushing one more side wave, or holding pressure can be correct when the enemy's position, respawn timers, and numbers prove that no punish actually exists. Fear-based play without map context gives away too much value.
actionables: Before backing off from a low-HP position, check whether the map actually contains a punish path.
Use enemy numbers, death timers, and visible positions to decide whether the extra wave is real greed or free value.
Review greedy-looking moments by asking whether the map proved them wrong or actually justified them.

source.title: Jugar asi me convirtio en la mejor camille de europa (te enseno como lo hice)
source.notes: Built from the low-HP push examples that were safe because all punish resources were on the other side of the map.
